model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Arkansas holds a slight edge in overall efficiency metrics, ranking 17th in KenPom and 20th in Torvik compared to Wisconsin's 22nd and 21st, with a top-5 adjusted offense that can exploit the Badgers' defense. While Wisconsin's offense is potent, Arkansas's experience in the tougher SEC and superior defensive rebounding provide a more reliable path to victory in this neutral-site matchup. The underdog's path for Wisconsin relies on hot three-point shooting and forcing turnovers, but Arkansas's balanced attack and efficiency metrics make them the more reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Efficiency and Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas holds a slight edge in advanced metrics and offensive firepower, while Wisconsin's defense is comparable but their offense is slightly less efficient.
reasoningStep.evidence: Arkansas ranks 17th in KenPom (adjO 127.6, adjD 102.1) and 20th in Torvik (adjO 127.8, adjD 102.0); Wisconsin ranks 22nd in KenPom (adjO 125.4, adjD 102.0) and 21st in Torvik (adjO 127.2, adjD 101.6). Arkansas's top-5 adjusted offense should challenge Wisconsin's defense, which is ranked 48th in Torvik.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

This week he discusses the latest (or lack there of) with AJ Brown, and some of the pending QB conversations around the NFL. Then, Hofstra HC Speedy Claxton joins to discuss his team making it to the NCAA Tournament, and how that feels for him as a former Hofstra player. Also, Wisconsin HC Greg Gard stops by to talk about how he feels heading in to round one of the NCAA Tournament. Plus, Former Hornet G Dell Curry joins to talk about his jersey going up into the rafters in Charlotte! Learn more about your ad choices. result.text: Arkansas Razorbacks Scores, Stats and Highlights - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search 14 Arkansas Razorbacks 26-8 2nd in SEC Home Schedule Statistics Roster Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge NEW: TC Eliminator 2025-26 Schedule Postseason vs 13 Hawai'i 3/19 4:25 pm TBS Regular Season vs Southern W 109-77 @ 22 Michigan St L 69-66 vs C Arkansas W 93-56 vs Samford W 79-75 vs Winthrop W 84-83 vs Jackson St W 115-61 vs 4 Duke L 80-71 vs 6 Louisville W 89-80 vs Fresno St W 82-58 vs 16 Texas Tech W 93-86 vs Queens W 108-80 vs 8 Houston L 94-85 vs James Madison W 103-74 vs 19 Tennessee W 86-75 @ Ole Miss W 94-87 @ Auburn L 95-73 vs South Carolina W 108-74 @ 21 Georgia L 90-76 vs 15 Vanderbilt W 93-68 vs LSU W 85-81 @ Oklahoma W 83-79 vs Kentucky L 85-77 @ Mississippi St W 88-68 @ LSU W 91-62 vs Auburn W 88-75 @ 25 Alabama L 117-115 vs Missouri W 94-86 vs Texas A&M W 99-84 @ 7 Florida L 111-77 vs Texas W 105-85 @ Missouri W 88-84 vs Oklahoma W 82-79 vs Ole Miss W 93-90 vs 22 Vanderbilt W 86-75 Full Schedule Jaylen Waddle Trade Reactions + Titans GM Mike Borgonzi Peter offers his perspective and instant analysis on the Broncos-Dolphins trade that brings Jaylen Waddle to Denver and leaves question marks in Miami. Then, Peter details just how much there was to the A.J. Brown-to-the-Rams rumors from a week ago. After some careless, reckless, and completely absurd NCAA Tournament thoughts, Peter brings in Titans GM Mike Borgonzi. They talk about the Titans' recent spending spree, which included a slew of new players and faces, and 17 additions to the roster. They go through what Robert Saleh and Brian Daboll are looking for in players and why so many former Jets and Giants were acquired in recent days. It's Cam Ward storyline, after that, with Borgonzi detailing what he's seen in their franchise QB over the course of the last eleven months. Then, Borgonzi goes down memory lane and takes us through his Kansas City days and what he remembers from scouting Travis Kelce and Patrick Mahomes. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices 14h Razorbacks NCAAM Hour 2: Overreaction Tuesday, plus ESPN’s Jay Bilas Previews the NCAA Tournament ESPN College Basketball Analyst Jay Bilas and Rich preview the 2026 NCAA Tournament including his prediction of Duke and Michigan losing in upsets, the Arizona Wildcats winning it all, why Arkansas’ Darius Acuff Jr. could steal the spotlight and take the Razorbacks on a deep run, reveals that he thinks America hates Michigan basketball because of Rich’ non-stop honking, and says why the pairing of Charles Barkley and Dick Vitale might be the greatest thing to happen to sportscasting in decades. Rich and Brockman debate which teams are likely to make the NFL playoffs next season and if the Patriots or Chargers will h

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r32-west-2
Current game label: West Round of 32 2
Round: Round of 32
Resolved teams: Wisconsin (wisconsin) vs Arkansas (arkansas)
Prior committed picks: 41
